All Patterns
Depth: Deep Model role: Reader and generator over retrieved context

RAG and Knowledge

Chunks, embeds, retrieves, and reranks documents to answer questions from specific knowledge

Real World Analogy

Like a lawyer preparing for court. Before arguing the case, the lawyer reviews thousands of documents, pulls the most relevant passages, organizes them by topic, and only then presents the argument. The quality of the prep work determines the quality of the argument.

How It Works

RAG harnesses let you give an AI access to your own documents so it can answer questions based on your specific information. The system breaks your documents into small pieces, converts them into numerical representations that capture their meaning, stores them in a searchable database, and retrieves the most relevant pieces when you ask a question.

The engineering challenge is almost entirely in the data pipeline. Getting the right information to the AI at the right time requires sophisticated document parsing that handles PDFs, spreadsheets, web pages, and dozens of other formats. Chunking strategies determine how documents are split, and the quality of this splitting directly affects answer quality.

More advanced systems build knowledge graphs that map relationships between concepts, enabling the AI to connect information across multiple documents. This multi hop reasoning produces richer answers than simple document retrieval alone.

Components

  • Document ingestion supporting dozens of file types and data sources
  • Chunking strategies from simple fixed size to semantic and layout aware
  • Embedding generation and vector storage for similarity search
  • Hybrid search combining keyword matching with semantic retrieval
  • Reranking to refine and prioritize retrieved results
  • Permission aware retrieval respecting enterprise access controls

Real World Examples

LlamaIndex

Framework with 150+ data connectors and multiple indexing strategies

Glean

Enterprise search connecting 100+ business apps with an organizational knowledge graph

Microsoft GraphRAG

Builds knowledge graphs from documents using AI to extract entities and relationships

Vectara

Fully managed RAG service with built in hallucination detection across 100+ languages

Key Terms

Chunking

Breaking documents into smaller pieces that fit within the AI's processing limits. How you chunk determines what AI can find.

Vector embedding

A list of numbers that represents the meaning of text. Similar texts produce similar patterns, enabling search by meaning.

Knowledge graph

A structured map of entities and relationships, enabling AI to reason across multiple documents.

Related Patterns

  • Embedding and Vector
  • Search and Answer
  • Conversational
